title: Asyncio Task Groups, Backpressure, Queues, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs asyncio task groups, backpressure, queues, cancellation, and cleanup from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for structured concurrent crawl engine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Own related crawl tasks in TaskGroup scopes and define how one failure cancels, aggregates, or isolates sibling work.
Bound frontier and result queues, state who can close them, and make producers wait or shed work under explicit policy.
Acquire global, origin, browser, and output capacity in one documented order and release every permit in finally paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Concurrent Determinism
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Concurrent Determinism: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review site-quality team handles case 7448 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate TaskGroup ownership, bounded queues, semaphore order, cancellation, cleanup, and stable result evidence; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate concurrent execution order from stable report order and test changed schedules, partial failure, and cancellation.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Concurrent Determinism.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Concurrent Determinism, keep Concurrent Determinism connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate concurrent execution order from stable report order and test changed schedules, partial failure, and cancellation.
